name: zowe-mcp-vscode
description: Zowe MCP Server for z/OS - provides AI tools for data sets, jobs, and UNIX System Services. Includes Zowe Dark and Zowe Light color themes using the Zowe design palette.
author: Zowe

zoweMCP.nativeSystems.description: Connection specs for Zowe Native (SSH) backend: user@hostname or user@hostname:port (e.g. USERID@sys1.example.com). When non-empty, the MCP server starts with the native backend. Passwords are stored in the shared Zowe secret namespace (zowe.ssh.password.*).
zoweMCP.nativeSystems.markdownDescription: Connection specs for Zowe Native (SSH) backend: `user@hostname` or `user@hostname:port` (e.g. `USERID@sys1.example.com`). When non-empty, the MCP server starts with the native backend. Passwords are stored in the shared Zowe secret namespace (`zowe.ssh.password.*`).

zoweMCP.logLevel.description: Minimum log level for the Zowe MCP Server. Changes take effect immediately without restarting.
zoweMCP.logLevel.markdownDescription: Minimum log level for the Zowe MCP Server (RFC 5424 levels: `debug`, `info`, `notice`, `warning`, `error`, `critical`, `alert`, `emergency`). Changes take effect immediately without restarting.

zoweMCP.installZoweNativeServerAutomatic.description: When true, automatically install the Zowe Native server on the host when "Server not found" is detected. Set to false to disable auto-install. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.
zoweMCP.installZoweNativeServerAutomatic.markdownDescription: When `true`, automatically install the Zowe Native server on the host when "Server not found" is detected. Set to `false` to disable auto-install. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.

zoweMCP.zoweNativeServerPath.patternErrorMessage: Must be a Unix-style path for z/OS UNIX System Services (e.g. ~/.zowe-server or /var/zowe-server)
zoweMCP.zoweNativeServerPath.description: Remote path on the z/OS host (UNIX System Services). Must start with / or ~. Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.
zoweMCP.zoweNativeServerPath.markdownDescription: Remote path on the z/OS host (UNIX System Services). Must start with `/` or `~` (e.g. `~/.zowe-server` or `/var/zowe-server`). Changes are sent to the server and apply to future connections.

zoweMCP.mockDataDirectory.patternErrorMessage: Must not contain control characters. Use an absolute path for your OS (e.g. /path, C:\path, or ~/path).
zoweMCP.mockDataDirectory.description: Local absolute path to a mock data directory. When set and Native Systems is empty, the MCP server starts in mock mode. Use the 'Zowe MCP: Generate Mock Data' command to create one. Leave empty to use native (SSH) mode. Changes require restarting the MCP server.
zoweMCP.mockDataDirectory.markdownDescription: Local absolute path to a mock data directory (e.g. `/path`, `C:\\path`, or `~/path`). When set and **Native Systems** is empty, the MCP server starts in mock mode. Use the **Zowe MCP: Generate Mock Data** command to create one. Leave empty to use native (SSH) mode. Changes require restarting the MCP server.

zoweMCP.defaultMainframeMvsEncoding.patternErrorMessage: Must be a valid IBM encoding format (e.g. IBM-037, IBM-1047)
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeMvsEncoding.description: Default mainframe encoding (EBCDIC) for dataset read/write (e.g. IBM-037, IBM-1047). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeMvsEncoding.markdownDescription: Default mainframe encoding (EBCDIC) for dataset read/write (e.g. `IBM-037`, `IBM-1047`). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.

zoweMCP.defaultMainframeUssEncoding.patternErrorMessage: Must be a valid IBM encoding format (e.g. IBM-1047)
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeUssEncoding.description: Default mainframe encoding for USS file operations (e.g. IBM-1047). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.
zoweMCP.defaultMainframeUssEncoding.markdownDescription: Default mainframe encoding for USS file operations (e.g. `IBM-1047`). Use the encoding used at your site. Changes are sent to the server and take effect immediately.

scripts.build:server: npm run build -w zowe-mcp-server && npm run bundle:server
scripts.build:all: npm run build:server && npm run build
scripts.build-and-install: npm run package && npm run install-extension
